dcrypt.askPasswordForFile.description: If enabled, the extension will prompt for a password when opening any `.pgp` or `.asc` file for the first time. If disabled, it will ask for one password for the user's session (which is just the name of your home directory).

dcrypt.useGpgKey.description: If enabled, the extension will use the GPG CLI for public-key encryption and decryption instead of symmetric passphrase-based encryption via OpenPGP.js. Requires GPG to be installed and the key to be in your keyring.

dcrypt.gpgKeyId.description: The GPG key identifier (fingerprint, key ID, or email) used as the recipient for encryption. The corresponding private key must be in your GPG keyring for decryption. Required when `dcrypt.useGpgKey` is enabled.

dcrypt.gpgPath.description: Path to the GPG binary. Override this if `gpg` is not on your system PATH.

scripts.pretest: npm run compile && npm run lint
